THOUGHT:
Analyzing the current citation list, I have strong coverage for system-level deployment challenges (Sculley et al., 2015), distribution shift/benchmarking (Al-Emadi et al., 2025), and uncertainty/calibration (Raposo & Umer, 2026). However, several key categories remain underrepresented, particularly "Using Specific Concepts" related to intrinsic model failure modes, "Supporting Arguments" for why models generalize poorly despite high benchmark scores, and "Highlighting Research Gaps" regarding dataset-induced biases. The most critical missing citation is a foundational paper on **shortcut learning and spurious correlations**, which directly explains a primary real-world pitfall: models latching onto superficial dataset statistics rather than learning robust, causal features.
